steps: The problem defines force as the product of mass and acceleration: $$F = m \cdot a$$
Substitute the given mass of 33 kg and acceleration $a$ m/s$^2$: $$F = 33 \cdot a = 33a$$

Evaluate each choice:
  • A: $F = 33a$ — Force equals mass times acceleration with mass = 33. This is exactly what the formula gives. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: $F = 33 + a$ — Uses addition instead of multiplication; the problem says "product," not sum. Eliminated.
  • C: $F = 33 - a$ — Uses subtraction; unrelated to the definition given. Eliminated.
  • D: $F = a - 33$ — Uses subtraction in the wrong order; unrelated to the definition given. Eliminated.
